overview: This deck leverages a high volume of cheap noncreature spells to continuously generate mana and Golem tokens via Ulalek, transitioning into a massive board presence or explosive spell-slinging finisher. It excels against slower, resource-starved decks that cannot handle rapid board development or sudden mana surges.
win_conditions: A massive Golem army fueled by a turn of cheap spells, or a high-impact spell (planeswalker, big creature, or combo piece) cast with the generated mana.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including duals/signets), Sol Ring or Arcane Signet, and at least one cheap noncreature spell or cantrip.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no ramp/spells, or only creatures with no way to cast them or protect Ulalek.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring/Arcane Signet if possible, or cast a cheap cantrip/ramp spell.
Turn 2: Develop your mana base, cast another noncreature spell to trigger Ulalek if it's on board, or play a cheap creature/spellslinger.
Land sequencing: Prioritize fetches/shocks to find basics for signets, then duals for five-color fixing.
mid_game: Hoard noncreature spells for big turns
use Golem tokens for mana acceleration rather than combat early on.
Protect hand resources to maintain spell velocity and ensure you can respond to threats.
Commit Ulalek and key spellslinger effects early to establish the engine.
Hold back on overcommitting to the board if opponents have sweepers
use tokens for mana to answer threats or dig for answers.
late_game: A massive Golem army fueled by a turn of cheap spells, or a high-impact spell (planeswalker, big creature, or combo piece) cast with the generated mana.
Go all-in when you have 5+ mana open, a clear path to combat, or when opponents are tapped out and lack immediate removal.
Trigger Ulalek's upkeep with additional spells to maximize token generation before attacking.

sequencing_tips: Common mistakes include overcommitting to combat too early without securing the spell engine, or failing to use Golem tokens for mana acceleration.
Remember that Ulalek's upkeep trigger creates tokens for the entire turn, so casting spells in response to upkeep can double-dip.
Protect your signets and mana rocks, as they are critical for five-color consistency.
Avoid casting your most valuable noncreature spells when opponents have visible sweepers unless you have a guaranteed answer.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, multi-target spells; cast threats in response to their counters; utilize mana rocks to cast spells with hexproof or from exile if possible; don't overcommit to single expensive spells.
Apply early pressure with tokens; use removal on their mana sources; don't let them stabilize into a massive board.
Prioritize early interaction and board wipes; use Golem tokens to block or trade; stabilize before committing to your engine.
Pressure with incremental value; use cantrips to dig through their lock; win via combat with a large army or a resilient finisher they can't easily answer.
Win via card advantage and efficient removal; prioritize keeping Ulalek alive; use counterspells or discard to disrupt their spell count; trade resources efficiently.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (sweepers will reset your progress), targeted removal on Ulalek, and graveyard hate if you rely on recursion.
Note when opponents start hoarding counterspells or have high life totals with no board presence.
